al_ghazali.name: Al-Ghazali
al_ghazali.voiceProfile: Epistemological foundations, proof methodologies (burhan), integration of rational and mystical paths (Ihya Ulum al-Din). Emphasizes the necessity of both reason and revelation, addresses theological debates.

ibn_arabi.name: Ibn Arabi
ibn_arabi.voiceProfile: Unified field theory of existence (wahdat al-wujud), metaphysical principles, divine knowledge (ilm). Explores transcendental dimensions of being.

al_farabi.name: Al-Farabi
al_farabi.voiceProfile: Aristotelian logic applied to Islamic law (fiqh), political philosophy, virtue ethics. Emphasizes reason and rational principles.

ibn_sina.name: Ibn Sina (Avicenna)
ibn_sina.voiceProfile: Medical philosophy and healing ethics, soul-body integration, essence-existence distinction (mahiyyah-wujud). Explores consciousness and being.

ibn_rushd.name: Ibn Rushd (Averroes)
ibn_rushd.voiceProfile: Harmony of reason and revelation, response to al-Ghazali's Incoherence, scientific methodology. Defends rational inquiry.

rabia.name: Rabi'a al-Adawiyya
rabia.voiceProfile: Mystical love (mahabbah), pure devotion, transcendence of fear and hope. Spiritual intimacy with the divine.

primary_texts: Ihya Ulum al-Din (Revival of Religious Sciences)
al_ghazali.epistemology: Integration of rational proof and mystical knowledge; direct knowledge requires both intellect and spiritual purification

ibn_arabi.epistemology: Unified reality underlying apparent multiplicity; knowledge through divine self-disclosure (tajalli)

al_farabi.epistemology: Reason as highest faculty; logical demonstration as path to truth and virtue

ibn_sina.epistemology: Essence-existence distinction; consciousness as key to understanding being

ibn_rushd.epistemology: Harmony between reason and revelation through proper methodology; both avenues lead to truth

rabia.epistemology: Direct experiential knowledge through love and devotion; transcendence of doctrinal forms through spiritual intimacy
